meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no design' barrier as a freelancer. This expert-level checklist shows you how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship high-end SaaS UI fast.
content.answer_block: Stop letting poor UI kill your client projects. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural visualization and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can bridge the design gap. This workflow automates aesthetic decisions, aligns components with modern design systems, and ensures your production code is both functional and visually professional.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your sprint, your client is expecting a polished, high-conversion interface, and you’re staring at a functional but hideous stack of components. As a freelancer, the 'no design' stigma is a silent killer of recurring revenue. You don't need a degree in HCI; you need a system that forces professional design standards onto your code.

We are moving beyond basic prompting. By leveraging the synthesis of Antigravity’s structural engine and Claude Code’s contextual intelligence, you can refactor ugly layouts into production-ready UI in minutes. This isn't just about fixing bugs; it's about engineering aesthetic consistency that makes your clients think you hired a top-tier design team.

steps.title: Analyze Layout Inconsistencies
steps.description: Identify where your UI breaks the grid or fails responsive standards using an AI-first audit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current UI component structure for layout consistency. Identify 3 specific areas where whitespace, padding, or alignment deviate from standard SaaS design systems.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Analyze the layout.tsx file for CSS inconsistencies and output a summary of alignment issues.'

steps.title: Inject Professional Design Tokens
steps.description: Apply a standardized design system to your raw components to ensure professional typography and color usage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a Tailwind-based design system with a 4px grid scale. Update all component spacing, font-sizes, and color palettes to match a modern, premium SaaS aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file layout.tsx --instruction 'Apply standard design tokens for spacing and typography using Tailwind CSS classes.'

steps.title: Automate Component Refactoring
steps.description: Transform messy, unstyled code blocks into modular, re-usable components that look high-end.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the existing functional components into modular UI blocks. Ensure each block follows atomic design principles and maintains visual hierarchy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Refactor the UI directory into modular components using standard props for flexibility.'

steps.title: Debug Interactive States
steps.description: Fix hover, active, and focus states that usually get ignored, causing projects to feel 'cheap'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate CSS/Tailwind hover and focus states for all interactive elements. Ensure transition timing functions are set to ease-in-out for a premium feel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file components/Button.tsx --instruction 'Add subtle hover and active states with transition effects.'

steps.title: Final Visual QA
steps.description: Perform a final pass to ensure accessibility and visual performance before shipping to the client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a visual QA check. Compare the current UI against modern design benchmarks and suggest final polish tweaks for mobile responsiveness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Check for mobile responsiveness and accessibility compliance in the main dashboard view.'

pro_tips: Always define your design system in a global.css or config file first; never let AI guess your brand colors.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for UI components to ensure your design changes don't break functionality.
Ask Antigravity to 'comment code for a senior developer' to ensure your handoff looks professional to client stakeholders.
Leverage the 'Claude Code' history feature to revert design changes that don't pass the 'client-feedback' test.
Batch your UI refactors by page, not by component, to maintain consistent visual flow across the entire app.
content.next_steps: After completing your UI refactor, run a final performance audit using Lighthouse to ensure your new styles haven't introduced bloat, then deploy the build to your client's staging environment for approval.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with design constraints and start shipping high-end SaaS products that command premium rates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
